title: crotalaria camptosepala
intro: Crotalaria camptosepala, a shrub-like plant species native to tropical Africa, is a member of the pea family (Fabaceae) and is also known as the 'African rattlebox'. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Crotalaria camptosepala include treating fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as an insect repellent. Crotalaria camptosepala can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine practices.
